data.title: Lamborghini CEO says Trump tariffs causing billionaire customers to delay purchases, facing 27.5% import duties.
data.description: Lamborghini Automobili's CEO Stephan Winkelmann has revealed that due to the tariff uncertainty caused by President Donald Trump, even billionaire customers are delaying their purchases of supercars. The Italian luxury brand is currently facing 27.5% duties on exports to the U.S. Winkelmann stated that some wealthy clients are holding off on buying as they await confirmation on the final tariff figures. He noted, "They are millionaires or billionaires for a reason, so they know what they're doing." This information highlights the impact of trade policies on high-end automakers, demonstrating how economic uncertainties can influence consumer behavior and purchasing decisions.

data.title: Earnings reports for Frontline: $0.50 per share, $321.73 million revenue; LandBridge: $0.71 per share, $169.13 million revenue.
data.description: Before the market opens, several companies are scheduled to report their quarterly earnings. Frontline (NYSE: FRO) is anticipated to announce earnings of $0.50 per share with revenue expected to be around $321.73 million. LandBridge (NYSE: LB) is projected to report earnings of $0.71 per share with revenue estimated at $169.13 million. Additionally, Alibaba Gr Hldgs (NYSE: BABA) earnings report can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Raoul Pal believes crypto market is poised for launch after consolidation phase. Altcoins, excluding BTC and ETH, are ready to break out.
data.description: Macro analyst Raoul Pal mentioned that various segments of the cryptocurrency market are currently in a consolidation phase or a "waiting room" stage, but they are displaying signs of potential breakouts. The total cryptocurrency market, excluding Bitcoin and Ethereum, is deemed by Pal to be on the verge of launching from this waiting period. He highlighted an Ascending Triangle pattern as evidence of strong buying demand. Additionally, Pal indicated that altcoins beyond the top 10 in terms of market capitalization are also in this waiting phase, although their launch may take more time. This analysis is available in detail on a finance news platform.

data.title: Hisense is dominating large-screen TV market, introduces RGB-MiniLED before IFA 2025, tops global shipments.
data.description: In Qingdao, China on August 29, 2025, Hisense, a top brand in the global consumer electronics and home appliances industry, is reinforcing its position as a leader in premium display technology. Recent data from Omdia for the first half of the year shows that Hisense has secured the top spot globally in shipments, underscoring its dominance in the market.

data.title: Van Eck Associates Corporation discloses holdings in Endeavour Mining plc as of 27 August 2025.
data.description: Endeavour Mining plc has disclosed that Van Eck Associates Corporation recently notified the Company of its holdings as of 27 August 2025. This notification pertains to Van Eck's current interests in the Company.

data.title: Analysts predict LDO price may hit $1.75-$2.10 in Sep 2025 despite current weakness, mixing signals with RSI at 46.47 & bearish MACD.
data.description: The Lido DAO is sending out conflicting signals, with the Relative Strength Index (RSI) currently standing at 46.47 and a bearish Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) indicator. Despite this, industry analysts are holding onto their optimistic outlook, predicting the price of Lido (LDO) to reach between $1.75 and $2.10 in the coming weeks.

data.title: Federal Reserve maintains outlook of two rate cuts in 2025, despite increased inflation forecasts. S&P 500 uncertain.
data.description: The Federal Reserve is maintaining its projection for two rate cuts in 2025 despite revising upwards its forecasts for inflation. The S&P 500 index, however, appears uncertain about this outlook.
